item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm tickled that the only legible word that one recovered human could write was adonai. after a long break from Judaism, I can't really read Hebrew, but adonai is the one word you never forget, that you don't even have to read along with the English phonetic spellings next to the Hebrew to recognize, because it's both ubiquitous and distinctive. that was a nice little detail that I really appreciated. adonai echad, amen!

for those who don't know — ADONAI is the Hebrew word for G-d.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hol' a moment, did the birds heal the d-guy's blindness?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was just trying to drill in they had weird Jesus fish manifesting powers. I did a calculation I forget now and that's what I got

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, maybe I just don't remember my Gospel Bill Show too good, but I couldn't follow any of this. Then just when it was starting to sort of gel near the end, the author literally dumped a giant pile of fish into the story. So I was like. Yeah. That's it. I'm out. I'm done. No voting action was taken because I don't feel qualified to judge this. Congrats on saving the deal-breaker for the very end, I guess? But literally, what the hell?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After an extended period of inactivity, the chamber containing SCP-2782 spontaneously manifested 52,561 fish. SCP-2782 appears ambivalent. Reasons for these manifestations are currently unknown.

I'm not sure, but maybe this has something to do with the "Syrian Hamster-AH-32", which apparently can manifest fish?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something bothers me here. The Kakapo Kerfluffle ocurred on March 13, 1996, yet the Wormwood entry earlier on is dated April 1996…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this in general, but the tone is too informal and some of the wording is really awkward or downright nonsensical.

"…incapacitated or otherwise deceased…"
You can be deceased but not incapacitated?

"As of now we know that D-0548 was innocent. We think SCP-2782 will ignore subjects it does not consider "good" in one way or another. We are not sure why he was not resurrected by SCP-2782, but it may be due to his behavior shortly after his resurrection."
So the guy was innocent, but the birds did NOT think he was good, and they didn't resurrect him because of how he acted after they resurrected him?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the atmosphere, but this seems like it would benefit from editing.

It feel too informal, and too unclear. I was definitely entertained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Updated this and two other articles (SCP-1616, SCP-1782) with a related articles crosslink.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like eggs.

item.title: Psych Birds
item.content:encoded: I hope this was intentional, but calling them "Green Doves" is insanely brilliant. if this isn't a reference to code greens, i don't know what is. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll concede to all of these, save for the image formatting. I really like the connected with white backgrounds for image series. I do it in SCP-1682 as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On a separate note: I LOVE THIS! Creepy, mysterious, weird. Wondering which critter got the transmute talent… Total +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would appear that the initial link is too wide to fit in the narrow space above, so it autoflows to the first spot it can appear full-width. When clicked, the phrase becomes MUCH smaller, allowing it to actually flow in the space assigned. Yay templates!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How the hell is that collapsible in reverse?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• I cleaned up the image blocks so they're at least using the new templates' styles if not the templates themselves. This at least makes them more consistent with the standard SCP template, but feel free to revert if you don't like the way it looks.
  • I strongly recommend either relocating or removing that first collapsible as it's interacting very badly with floating elements such as the image blocks. If it is essential for containment, then I don't see why it needs to be hidden behind a collapsible. If it isn't, then as "Supplementary Material" it should probably be in an addendum at the end of the Description block. Either way, it looks really ugly as it is.
  • I uploaded a downsized version of the second image that isn't 1.25mb and switched the image blocks over to using the non-sandbox version of the images. Please delete the original image if you don't need it here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish this had been clearer when I saw it last night. I upvoted anyway, but I mean, holy crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice work, and the inscription with slovak numerals gained you personal brownie points.
Ultimately, this is where I upvoted

The birds are extremely holy. I can hardly understand their faces anymore. Are they of Christ? Or are they simply his cherubim?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is what I want to see.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Surprising how the lab rat is actually considered the most dangerous of the three scps made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ezekiel is the old man in 1782. He is also the person in this article who is responsible for the "experiments". He is an unwitting reality bender and the results of his experiments are merely an exertion of his own will, not some newly discovered "Holy Science".

The reality bender, Daniel, was supposed to be the Begotten, but he was aborted midterm by his mother. He is twisted, not what he could have been. Ezekiel now exists more or less in Daniel's subconscious as of the events of 1782, as does his mother.

Wormwood, or Nibbles (1616) is just a lab rat with a specific trait of "the Christ", in that he can perform the miracle of gathering.

The birds can perform the miracle of resurrection, healing, and water walking.

Daniel was supposed to be able to perform all miracles and bring about the Second Advent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Might be better to have the time as UTC rather than a regional NZST for scientific purposes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I get this, 1616 is the fearless predatory hamster offspring released into the wild and then enhanced by Ezekiel and the birds in 1782 are successful experiments trapped and perverted by Ezekiel with some random old man. Doesn't explain the aborted reality bender, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh Faminepulse, how I love thee. And your writing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit the formatting looks really cool to me for some reason! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+Spoilers and shtuff
-Shpoiled

So this wacky, overzealous reality bender done mucked up and created three separate skips just by "doing the Lord's work?" Huh. Here, take a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So is this the reason why that old man in 1782 was getting devoured by birdies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes that is exactly what this is. They are zealots trying to bring about the Second Advent with science. The birds are discarded like you discard nuclear waste. They were just stepping stones to the human Subject Zero.

The only thing I'm not telling you is

Ezekiel, the lead researcher and bishop
– hide block

doesn't know he's a reality bender and his Holy Science is just an exertion of his will. But that's not really important.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do not completely understand what is going on here. I hate to be that guy, but will you post up some spoilers in a collapsible?

+But, before you do, here is my theory on what is going on.
-Was I hot or cold?

Someone at this New Zealand research facility was performing genetic experiments in order to make better, more human test subjects, as well as to help improve the breeding rates of certain endangered species. Religious zealots took over the facility and attempted to use the results of the first experiments to bring about the Second Coming of Christ, but something went wrong and they attempted to abort the experiment, resulting in the weird birds that now inhabit the basement of the facility.


I'll read the SCPs this is supposed to be an origin story for to see if I can gain some insight, but until I think I have a solid grasp on what's going on, this is a novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Djoric and Zolgomax (and Djkaktus literally 4 minutes before posting) for reading this and offering feedback.

This is backstory to SCP-1616 and SCP-1782, because Fantem did something like this and, well, fuck.
